title: AEP v1.1 A2 Lesson Kernel
description: v1.1 A2 amendment (STRONGLY PLAUSIBLE × CONTEXT-BOUND PATTERN). Two-agent convergence (scribe + strategist). <=200-token irreducible nucleus that survives context compaction. The kernel is the operator-readable answer to 'if you could only carry one sentence forward, what would it be?'. Composes_with: sec02 truth-tags (kernel inherits parent lesson's tag), v1.0.3 RegexicalCue (kernels MAY be cue-anchored).

kernel_text.description: The kernel itself. MUST be <=200 tokens (validator approximates via 4-char/token heuristic; canonical token counter is operator-config-bound).

kernel_token_count.description: Token count under the canonical tokenizer. Validator EMITS AEP11_A2_KERNEL_TOO_LARGE if >200.

kernel_sha256.description: sha256 over UTF-8 bytes of kernel_text. Used for compaction-survival audit.

compaction_survival_test_at.description: Last time the kernel was tested under blind-compaction (operator query without parent lesson loaded).

compaction_survival_score_0_to_5.description: Empirical recall score under blind compaction test. Composes with v1.0.3.1 F14 RubricScore.

anchored_to_regexical_cue_id.description: Optional. RegexicalCue id this kernel anchors to (kernel-as-recall-payload pattern).

examples.kernel_text: v1.0.3 RegexicalCue HARD-CONDITIONAL: 3 raters got 4.00 / 3.00 / 3.33; root cause = rubric definitional gap on list-valued recall; F14 + A4 backport closes mechanically; F12-F18 stack proceeds as v1.1.
